notes: Roman general and statesman known for his wealth, luxury, and military command during the late Republic.

docs.title: Life of Lucullus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … severity, that a man of not only incorrupt and just behavior, but also of moderation, should be employed in so heavy and odious an office. The Mitylenaeans, who absolutely revolted, he was willing should return to their duty, and submit to a moderate penalty for the offense they had given

docs.title: Letter 7040
docs.author: Quintus Aurelius Symmachus
docs.subject_summary:
Nondum Neapolitanum litus accessimus visuri arcem deliciamm tuarum, sed ta- 
men omnia, quae Tyrrhenus adluit, nominis tui plena sunt. quid multa? successisti 
in famam Luculli. qao magis miror, ad al
docs.excerpt: … the fame of Lucullus [the famously extravagant Roman general whose Neapolitan villas were legendary]. Which makes me all the more surprised you can ever bring yourself to leave. Unless you flee abundance out of fastidiousness, curing satiation with variety.
